events.name: Atlanta Falcons at Kansas City Chiefs

headlines.description: — Kansas City clinched the top seed in the AFC and the only playoff bye when Patrick Mahomes threw a 25-yard touchdown pass to Demarcus Robinson with just under two minutes for a 17-14 win over Atlanta on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chiefs clinch No. 1 seed when Falcons' Koo misses late FG

events.name: Cleveland Browns at New York Jets

headlines.description: — Baker Mayfield coughed up the ball — and the Cleveland Browns' chances to wrap up a playoff spot.
headlines.shortLinkText: Browns' playoff chances take hit with 23-16 loss to Jets

headlines.description: — The Pittsburgh Steelers are AFC North champions. Finally.
headlines.shortLinkText: Steelers rally past Colts to end skid, lock up AFC North

headlines.description: — The Jacksonville Jaguars took care of business against Chicago on Sunday, losing their 14th consecutive game and moving a step closer to locking up the No. 1 pick in the 2021 draft.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bears close in on playoffs, Jags close in on No. 1 pick

events.name: New York Giants at Baltimore Ravens

headlines.description: — Lamar Jackson directed four scoring drives during the decisive first half, and the surging Baltimore Ravens beat the New York Giants 27-13 Sunday to gain control of their own fate in the AFC playoff chase.
headlines.shortLinkText: Torrid Ravens get jump on Giants, roll to easy 27-13 victory

headlines.description: — Samaje Perine ran for two touchdowns, including a 3-yard score late, to give the Cincinnati Bengals their first road win in more than two years with a 37-31 victory over the Houston Texans on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bengals beat Texans 37-31 for first road win since 2018

events.name: Denver Broncos at Los Angeles Chargers

headlines.description: — Jon Gruden's red-faced resignation didn't unravel the Las Vegas Raiders. It galvanized them.
headlines.shortLinkText: Raiders begin post-Jon Gruden era with 34-24 win at Denver

headlines.description: — Dwayne Haskins turned the ball over three times before getting benched, Steven Sims muffed a punt return that turned into a Panthers touchdown and Washington blew its first chance to clinch the NFC East by losing to Carolina 20-13 Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Washington beaten by Panthers, fails to clinch NFC East

headlines.description: — Andy Dalton threw for 377 yards and three touchdowns, two to Michael Gallup, and the Dallas Cowboys stayed alive in the playoff race with a 37-17 victory over the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cowboys beat Eagles 37-17, stay alive with Washington loss

events.name: Los Angeles Rams at Seattle Seahawks

headlines.description: — The Seattle Seahawks claimed the NFC West title with a 20-9 win over the Los Angeles Rams on Sunday, with Russell Wilson throwing a 13-yard TD pass to Jacob Hollister with 2:51 left for the clinching score.
headlines.shortLinkText: Seahawks wrap up NFC West title with 20-9 win over Rams

events.name: Tennessee Titans at Green Bay Packers

headlines.description: — Davante Adams caught three of Aaron Rodgers’ four touchdown passes as the Green Bay Packers trounced the Tennessee Titans 40-14 for their fifth consecutive victory on a snowy Sunday evening.
headlines.shortLinkText: Adams shines in snow as Packers trounce Titans 40-14
